claim: There is no simple graph L on 18 labeled vertices that is 7-regular, triangle-free, and has independence number at most 7.
application: This excludes the minimum-degree-five equality endpoint in the P(8,3) reduction recorded in R8_DEPENDENCY_LEDGER.md.
nonclaims: This package alone does not prove P(8,3).
This package alone does not prove the fixed r=8 case of Erdos Problem 617.
This package does not prove the universal statement in Erdos Problem 617.
The package has not been released or externally reviewed.

formula.edge_variable_map: Lexicographic pairs (u,v), 0 <= u < v < 18, mapped to variables 1,...,153.
formula.symmetry_break: N_L(0)={1,...,7}; this is safe because every proposed graph is 7-regular.
formula.meaning: Each vertex has degree exactly 7, each triple omits at least one edge, and each eight-set contains an edge.

semantic_audit.method: Independent clause-multiset reconstruction plus inductive threshold audit of every totalizer merge.

optional_raw_derivation.note: The checked LRAT is the preserved proof artifact; the earlier DRAT derivation is not required for replay.
